summary: Tested all 3 P3 features (Batch Cert, Audit Export, Smart Contract Escrow). 19/19 backend pytest passed. Batch Certificates page renders 36 sealed ceremonies + filter + Select-all + Download ZIP. Admin Dashboard → Audit Logs tab renders Batch Cert link card AND AuditExportPanel with date filters + Preview + Export ZIP (34 rows) + History. Hash chain verified byte-for-byte: every row's row_hash == sha256(prev_hash || canonical_json(row\{hashes})) and rows[-1].row_hash == export root_hash. Found one Smart Contract state-machine inconsistency bug.

minor.issue: After a successful /deposit, the contract-state endpoint reports state='DRAFT' instead of 'FUNDED'. Root cause: /deposit sets financial.deposit_status='held' but does NOT change escrow.status (still 'draft'). The contract-state reconcile map at escrow_routes.py:1296 maps status='draft' -> 'DRAFT' regardless of deposit_status. The smart_contract.state field written by /deposit ('FUNDED') is overwritten by the reconcile. FUND opcode IS pushed correctly to operations[] and balance_usd is correct, but the surfaced state is wrong. Fix options: (a) /deposit also sets escrow.status='active'; (b) reconcile maps status='draft' + deposit_status='held' -> 'FUNDED'; (c) trust stored smart_contract.state when status='draft' and an explicit FUND op is present.

When the full audit_logs collection is exported (no date filter), the request times out at the kubernetes ingress (502 after ~60s). Hedera HCS submit + reportlab-free pure-Python hashing of all rows is slow for large datasets. Recommend either streaming the response (StreamingResponse + chunked ZIP) or rejecting unfiltered exports above N rows.

design_issues.screen: Admin Dashboard → Audit Logs → SOC2 Export panel
issues: Uses native HTML5 date inputs (mm/dd/yyyy) instead of shadcn Calendar component. Consistent with rest of admin but worth noting per QA design rules.

action_items: Fix Smart Contract state reconciliation: ensure /deposit either sets escrow.status='active' OR update reconcile map at escrow_routes.py:1296 so deposit_status='held' yields state='FUNDED' even when escrow.status='draft'. This is the only blocker for the SmartContractPanel UI showing the correct state machine position after fund.
(Optional) Audit export: cap row count or stream the ZIP to avoid 502 on unfiltered exports.

(Optional) Test SmartContractPanel UI rendering manually — backend contract-state contract was fully validated; couldn't auto-click escrow card in time but page route is wired in EscrowDashboard.jsx (per request).
critical_code_review_comments: escrow_routes.py is 1338 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting smart-contract layer (_mint_mock_contract, _append_contract_op, /contract-state, /refund) into a new escrow_smart_contract_routes.py module.

audit_export_routes.py L89: end_date concatenation 'T23:59:59.999999+00:00' assumes ISO timestamps stored as strings; if any audit_log row stored timestamp as datetime object the $lte comparison will misbehave. Consider normalising before filter.

admin_certs_routes.py L173 _safe_filename: not truly safe against double-dots/leading-dash; reportlab.platypus also doesn't escape document_name in cover PDF — low risk (admin-only) but worth a Paragraph escape pass.

escrow_routes.py /refund L1190 'No funds held' check relies on deposit_status=='held'; if a future flow has partial release (amount_held>0 but deposit_status='partial'), refund would be incorrectly blocked. Consider checking amount_held>0 directly.

Both new routes register set_db side-effect modules at import — standard pattern but make sure server.py also wires audit_log_exports collection index (e.g. on export_id) for the /history endpoint at scale.

success_rate.frontend: Batch Cert page and Audit Export Panel verified visually — not all DOM selectors clicked

test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! works. demo@test.com / Demo123! works (escrow_intelligence requires Enterprise so used admin for /escrow/create).
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST escrow (title TEST_p3_smart_contract escrow, $250). Funded and refunded as part of test. 1 audit_log_export row recorded.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 P3 features functionally work end-to-end on backend. Frontend renders correctly for Batch Cert page (/admin/batch-certificates) and Audit tab. The one functional bug is the state-machine mapping in /contract-state after /deposit (state stays DRAFT instead of FUNDED) — fix in escrow_routes.py:1296-1303. The refund flow itself works (returns 200, sets status=refunded, state=REFUNDED, pushes REFUND op).
rca of the issue: Smart Contract state bug RCA: (1) /escrow/{id}/deposit sets smart_contract.state='FUNDED' via $set and pushes FUND op via $push to smart_contract.operations — these writes DO persist correctly in MongoDB. (2) /escrow/{id}/contract-state reads back the escrow, then OVERWRITES the persisted smart_contract.state by mapping escrow.status (which is still 'draft' since /deposit never touched it) through status_to_state dict at line 1296-1303 which has no 'draft + deposit_status=held' branch. (3) Reconcile result is returned to client, masking the correctly-stored FUNDED state. Reproduction: POST /escrow/create -> POST /{id}/deposit -> GET /{id}/contract-state — expected state=FUNDED, actual state=DRAFT. Mitigation: add 'if escrow.status=="draft" and financial.deposit_status=="held": state="FUNDED"' branch, OR set escrow.status='active' inside deposit_funds().
